title: cinchona × boliviana
intro: Cinchona × boliviana, a hybrid plant derived from the cross between Cinchona ledgeriana and Cinchona officinalis, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which contains quinine, a natural antimalarial compound used to treat malaria and other fever-related conditions. The health benefits of Cinchona × boliviana include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate symptoms of malaria, and provide relief from various other infections. Cinchona × boliviana can be prepared as a herbal medicine in various forms, including teas, tinctures, and capsules, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
